supersedes: Prior run at 2026-07-07T12:30:00Z halted with overallStatus=FAILED because plan-catalog/docs/README.md did not exist. It has since been created and defines the canonical source hierarchy and approved canonical directory (docs/canonical/). This run proceeds past that halt.

checks.expected: File exists and defines source-governance rules
checks.actual: Exists (46 lines). Defines a 6-level source precedence hierarchy (Golden Fixture v3 > referenced versioned rule files > Plan Generation Decisions docs > reconciled architecture specs > brief > existing catalog/tests) and names docs/canonical/ as the sole approved canonical directory.

checks.check: docs/canonical/ directory exists and is listable
Both JSON fixtures use fixtureKey GOLDEN_10K_INTERMEDIATE_4D_12W

checks.source: golden-10k-intermediate-4d-12w.v3.plandocument.json $.contentHash vs recomputed SHA-256 using PlanCatalog.Infrastructure's actual canonical JSON conventions (System.Text.Json, JsonNode raw-number preservation, ordinal-ascending key sort, null-omit, JavaScriptEncoder.UnsafeRelaxedJsonEscaping, non-indented, NFC, contentHash field excluded)
checks.actual: 7b2e92dce611ae27751524ee41cb8c1b6b2b153eea7a125edbf5ab60eb3bc735 — EXACT MATCH. The earlier INCONCLUSIVE result (2026-07-07T12:30:00Z run) used a Node.js JSON.stringify reproduction, which normalizes numeric literals (e.g. 24.0 -> 24) and defaults to strict \uXXXX escaping; neither matches. Re-verified with a throwaway .NET console harness reusing System.Text.Json.Nodes.JsonNode (which preserves the source's raw numeric token text) plus JavaScriptEncoder.UnsafeRelaxedJsonEscaping — i.e. exactly PlanCatalog.Infrastructure.Serialization.CanonicalJsonOptions.Canonical's own encoder choice. That combination reproduces the stored hash exactly. Four variants were tried (default/relaxed escaping x indented/non-indented); only non-indented+relaxed-escaping matched.
8 (RACE_PACE_REPEATS) + 8 (EASY_STANDARD) + 4 (EASY_SHAKEOUT) = 20 km training; +10 km race = 30 km total

checks.check: Fixture template/rulePack artifact-version parity vs. current pilot catalog
checks.expected: N/A — recorded as two separate, non-blocking conclusions per explicit instruction
checks.actual: SOURCE_SEMANTICS_USABLE: the fixture's field-level domain semantics (phase weeks, week boundaries, peak-volume typical band, taper distribution, race accounting, vocabulary values, progression percentages) do not depend on which exact template/rule-pack version number is stamped on the document, and may still be used as evidence for the current v1 pilot artifacts where the underlying domain fact is version-independent. ARTIFACT_VERSION_PARITY_UNRESOLVED: the fixture's $.template.version=2 and $.rulePack.version=3 do not match the catalog's current TEN_K_MASTER v1 / APPSEL_RACE_PLAN_V1 v1, and this review does not upgrade, clone, or rename any catalog artifact to force parity. Any fixture field whose meaning is inherently tied to the specific v2/v3 artifact revision (e.g. a hypothetical v2-only phase or rule not present in v1) is NOT usable as evidence for the v1 artifact and must be flagged separately per-field if encountered.
